title: senecio pinnatifolius var. serratus
intro: Senecio pinnatifolius var. serratus, commonly known as Mountain Ragwort or Serrate Leaf Ragwort, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its serrated leaves and yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Senecio pinnatifolius var. serratus include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making a tea or infusion from its leaves, which can be used externally or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
